answer:
To find the limit, we can start by rationalizing the expression inside the parentheses.

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)$$

First, we can multiply the expression by its conjugate over itself to rationalize the numerator:

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\frac{\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}+\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)}{\sqrt{n^{3}-3}+\sqrt{n^{3}-2}}\right)$$

Simplifying the numerator, we get:

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\frac{(n^{3}-3)-(n^{3}-2)}{\sqrt{n^{3}-3}+\sqrt{n^{3}-2}}\right)$$

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\frac{-3+2}{\sqrt{n^{3}-3}+\sqrt{n^{3}-2}}\right)$$

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\frac{-1}{\sqrt{n^{3}-3}+\sqrt{n^{3}-2}}\right)$$

Now, we can simplify the expression by multiplying the numerator and denominator by the conjugate of the denominator:

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\frac{-1}{\sqrt{n^{3}-3}+\sqrt{n^{3}-2}} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}}{\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}}\right)$$

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\frac{-\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)}{\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}\right)^{2}-\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)^{2}}\right)$$

Simplifying the denominator, we get:

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\frac{-\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)}{n^{3}-3-n^{3}+2}\right)$$

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\frac{-\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)}{-1}\right)$$

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sqrt{n(n+1)(n+2)}\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)$$

Now, we can factor out a $n^2$ from the first term in the expression:

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} n^{2}\sqrt{\frac{(n+1)(n+2)}{n}}\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)$$

As $n$ approaches infinity, the expression inside the square root approaches 1:

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} n^{2}\left(\sqrt{n^{3}-3}-\sqrt{n^{3}-2}\right)$$

Now, we can factor out a $n^{3/2}$ from the first term:

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} n^{3/2}\left(\sqrt{1-\frac{3}{n^{3}}}-\sqrt{1-\frac{2}{n^{3}}}\right)$$

As $n$ approaches infinity, the expressions inside the square roots approach 1:

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} n^{3/2}\left(1-\frac{3}{n^{3}}-1+\frac{2}{n^{3}}\right)$$

Simplifying the expression, we get:

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} n^{3/2}\left(-\frac{1}{n^{3}}\right)$$

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \frac{n^{3/2}}{n^{3}}$$

$$\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} n^{-3/2}$$

As $n$ approaches infinity, $n^{-3/2}$ approaches 0.
